fields.headingSection: Thank you for your interest in the Remote Support Buyer's Guide
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted: Whether you're looking for a quick summary of the guide, the full report, or actionable guidance, find all Remote Support Buyer’s Guide resources available for download below.

fields.headingSection: Executive Summary: Key Takeaways from the Remote Support Buyer's Guide
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
Short on time? (5 minute read)

Whether you're a small IT business owner or part of a large enterprise organization's technical support team, choosing the right remote support software is pivotal to the productivity and security of your service desk. Access the Executive Summary now for key considerations for your next remote support solution.
fields.label: Download Summary

fields.headingSection: Complete Guide: Buyer’s Guide for Remote Support
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
Looking for a deep dive? (30 minute read)

In this guide, you'll discover:

  • The factors shaping the evolution of ITSM and the service desk, and why it matters
  • The essential remote support capabilities to prioritize
  • How consolidating to one remote support tool enterprise-wide will enable you to realize time-savings, security, auditing, cost-savings, and other IT support benefits.
  • What differentiates BeyondTrust Remote Support from the competition, and why it earned the distinction of "Customers' Choice"
fields.label: Download Full Guide

title: BeyondTrust Evaluation Agreement Standard Terms & Conditions

fields.headingSection: BeyondTrust Evaluation Agreement Standard Terms & Conditions

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
This Agreement is between Evaluator and BeyondTrust Corporation, a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business located at 11695 Johns Creek Parkway, Suite 200, Johns Creek, GA 30097 (“BeyondTrust”). Evaluator desires to evaluate and test (the “Evaluation”) the applicable BeyondTrust products as described above or in the electronic confirmation from BeyondTrust (the “Products)”, at no charge during the Evaluation Period as defined above, or in the electronic confirmation. The cover page is a material part of this Agreement.

1. License Grant. BeyondTrust hereby grants to Evaluator a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable right and license (“License”) to conduct the Evaluation during the Evaluation Period. The Evaluation of the Products will be for Evaluator’s internal evaluation purposes only. All products shall remain the property of BeyondTrust. Evaluator may not copy, modify or make derivative works of the Products, including customization, translation or localization, nor merge the Products with other computer programs nor reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile, nor attempt to derive the software source code. Evaluator agrees and acknowledges that the Products are provided to be used only for lawful purposes and in accordance with applicable laws.

2. Confidentiality. Unless otherwise agreed in a separate confidentiality agreement between the parties, Evaluator agrees to keep the Software (which shall include any technical specifications provided by BeyondTrust for the use and operation of the Software) confidential and not to disclose or make available the Software to any third party.

3. Limitation of Liability. Except for evaluator’s violation of section 1 above, to the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, (a)neither party will be liable for any indirect, special, incidental, punitive or consequential damages (including for the indirect loss of profit, revenue or content) arising out of or in connection with this agreement, however caused, and under whatever cause of action or theory of liability brought (including under any contract, negligence or other tort theory of liability) even if such party has been advised of the possibility of such damages; and (b) each party’s cumulative, aggregate liability for any damages shall not exceed the fees paid by evaluator to BeyondTrust for the evaluation giving rise to such liability.

4. Limited Warranty. BeyondTrust warrants that it can grant the License described in this Agreement. Since evaluator is receiving a license to conduct the evaluation at no charge, the products are provided strictly on an “as is” and unsupported basis and without any direct or implied warranty of any kind, including, without limitation, the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement.

5. Return or Destruction of Materials. Upon completion of the Evaluation, Evaluator agrees to cease all use of the Products and to remove and destroy the software and all copies thereof and, at BeyondTrust’s request certify in writing to their removal and destruction. If any hardware has been included with the Products, Evaluator agrees to promptly return the hardware to BeyondTrust at the end of the Evaluation Period at the expense of the Evaluator. Evaluator agrees that if for any reason the hardware is not returned to BeyondTrust in good condition except for normal wear and tear after the end of the Evaluation Period, Evaluator will be responsible for the cost of repairing or replacing the hardware.

6. Term Termination. Unless otherwise provided in a Product Description or terminated sooner, the term of the Agreement shall be for thirty days (30) from the date BeyondTrust delivers the product to Evaluator or otherwise make the software available for download. This Agreement shall terminate (i) immediately upon the breach by the Evaluator of BeyondTrust’s intellectual property rights, or (ii) five (5) days after written notice from a party to the other party in the event that breaching party has breached or violated any other provision of this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within such five (5) day period.

7. Entire Agreement. This Agreement will be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware. The parties agree and consent to personal jurisdiction and service and venue in any federal or state court within the State of Georgia. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter contained herein, and supersedes all prior representations, proposals, discussions and communications, whether oral or in writing. This Agreement may be modified only by a writing signed by both parties.

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
After a hugely successful 2023 event in Miami, our customers and partners asked us if we could regionalise to make it even more accessible to your employees. So, that’s exactly what we did.

Go Beyond 2024 will be held at the following locations:

fields.heading: Napa
fields.body: April 4th, 2024

fields.heading: Sydney
fields.body: April 11th, 2024

fields.heading: London
fields.body: April 23rd, 2024

fields.heading: Miami
fields.body: April 30rd - May 1st, 2024

fields.heading: Virtual
fields.body: April 30rd - May 1st, 2024 (live from Miami)

fields.heading: Dubai
fields.body: May 21st, 2024

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
We are also handling sponsorships very differently this year. We want to weave our partners into the overall event and be able to offer more value by focusing on our strategic regional plays and getting them the airtime, they want. That’s why this year, you are receiving a different prospectus. This isn’t a numbers game. We aren’t getting as many sponsors as we can. We want to showcase you in the best possible way.

See below for our unique proposal, just for you.

fields.headingSection: Global System Integrator Package: Keynote Sponsorship
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
You’ll be taking main stage in the morning to help deliver an educational 30 minute keynote on the regional market. The aim is to provide our audience with unbiased insights into the evolving identity market space. Emphasizing the important of cloud adoption in the broader context of security.

Keynote Overview

These are the key points we'd like you to cover:

  • Global Trends: Share insights into current global trends shaping the identity and security market landscape.
  • Cloud-Centric Approach: Highlight the increasing significance of cloud-based solutions and their role in the future of cybersecurity.
  • Best Practices: Discuss emerging best practices for implementing identity-first security approaches.
  • Technology Overview: Provide an overview of the latest technologies influencing advancements in identity security.
  • Case Studies: Share relevant success stories to illustrate practical applications.

The Outcome

Your keynote should empower BeyondTrust customers with a comprehensive understanding of the industry, helping them to make informed decisions about security strategies. Your impartial insights will contribute to shaping the community's knowledge base and encourage the adoption of identity-first security in the cloud.

Added Bonus: 5k Offer

A VIP lunch with your chosen customers from the day. We’ll share the customer lists and work with our Sales team to get the customers secured. We’ll be targeting 8-10 customers with 2-4 BT and GSI reps for engagement. This offer is only available to the keynote GSI sponsorship.

Join us around the world as we showcase the future of Identity Security. As a strategic partner of BeyondTrust, we’re excited to welcome you on the journey where innovation meets collaboration, paving the way for a secure world.

If you’d like to join us, please email partner.marketing@beyondtrust.com or let your Partner Manager know. We’d love to have you involved with our brand-new Go Beyond Roadshow event.

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
We are also handling sponsorships very differently this year. We want to weave our partners into the overall event and be able to offer more value by focusing on our strategic regional plays and getting them the airtime, they want. That’s why this year, you are receiving a different prospectus. This isn’t a numbers game. We aren’t getting as many sponsors as we can. We want to showcase you in the best possible way.

See below for our unique proposal, just for you.

You’ll help bring our content to life. We want you to either deliver a 15 minute live session or a pre recorded video on our integration centered around one pivotal customer story. The objective is to showcase integration innovation, emphasizing the real world impact on security solution.

These sessions should provide a concise but detailed overview of the customer’s transformation, with a call-to-action at the end driving the audience to see your demo. You'll take main stage in the morning to help deliver an educational keynote on the regional market. The aim is to provide our audience with unbiased insights into the evolving identity market space and to emphasize the importance of cloud adoption in security.

Keynote Overview

These are the key points we’d like you to cover.

  • Customer Security Journey: Dive into their security challenges, setting the stage for understanding the necessity of innovative integrations.
  • Integration Showcase: Explore how BeyondTrust and yourselves collaborated to address the customers’ needs, highlighting the key integrations that played a pivotal role.
  • Tangible Results: Show measurable outcomes and improvements achieved through the integrated solutions, focusing on the positive impact on security posture and operational efficiency.
  • Technical Insights: Provide concise technical insights into the integrations, offering enough detail for non-technical attendees to grasp the key functionalities without overwhelming them with technical jargon.
  • Live Demo Station: Direct attendees to a dedicated demo station outside the session room, where they can experience the showcased integration in action. Highlight the opportunity for firsthand interaction with the integrated solution.

The Outcome

This focused session aims to captivate the C-level audience with a deep dive into one customer's security journey, showcasing the tangible benefits of integration innovation. By concentrating on a single success story, attendees will gain a comprehensive understanding of the transformative power of seamless integrations. The live demo station provides an immediate and interactive extension of the session, allowing attendees to explore the showcased integration in real-time

Added Bonus: 1k Chair Drop

If you’d like a piece of your collateral on the seats of attendees before your session, please include this added bonus on your request.

Join us around the world as we showcase the future of Identity Security. As a strategic partner of BeyondTrust, we’re excited to welcome you on the journey where innovation meets collaboration, paving the way for a secure world.

If you’d like to join us please email partner.marketing@beyondtrust.com or let your Partner Manager know. We’d love to have you involved with our brand-new Go Beyond Roadshow event.

title: Demo New Video
GoBeyond Masterclass Recap - Remote Support

fields.headingSection: Thank you for attending our Remote Support masterclass!
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted: Download a take-home recap of session topics with relevant technical documentation.
fields.label: Download PDF

fields.headingSection: Cyberattacks on Service Desks are Rising
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted: Just this year, there have been multiple reports of breaches specifically targeting service desks (including recent incidents impacting AnyDesk and ConnectWise). More than ever before, service desks are in the sights of hackers and malicious players as a means to quickly get access to privileged or admin-level accounts, giving them the power to take down entire systems or demand multi-million dollar ransoms.

fields.headingSection: Immediate Steps to Secure Your Service Desk
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
Improving the security posture of your organization can sometimes be a huge undertaking. However, there are direct and indirect steps that you can take today to see immediate results in your service desk’s security posture.

The first direct step would be to immediately secure and protect all user and account credentials, effectively eliminating the need for unsafe password storage or sharing. The second step to indirectly bolster your service desk’s security would be to increase the efficiency of your workflows with high quality integrations. This prevents users from creating their own backdoor processes or workarounds.

fields.headingSection: Protect User & Account Credentials with the Vault
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
Beyond encouraging users to create secure passwords for accounts, it’s critical admins choose service desk solutions that enable secure user and account credential best practices.

The BeyondTrust Vault is a credential store that exists on the Secure Remote Access appliance, and is automatically included with every Remote Support deployment. It’s a fantastic, comprehensive solution for those without a secrets management solution or those who are not yet ready for an enterprise-level password vault like Password Safe.

The BeyondTrust Vault now stores up to 100,000 account credentials, and can discover, scan, and import Active Directory and local accounts. Console users will also be able to leverage credential injection to mitigate the risk of shared privileged account credentials. Users will be able to login without ever exposing their passwords on screen.

In addition to centralizing and refining password and accounts management processes, the Vault comes with its own Vault Account Policies management, including reporting to view and track credential activity, including the use of shared credentials.

Discover more about how the Vault works in our BeyondTrust Remote Support Vault Guide.

Check out any other included features for Remote Support you may not be fully leveraging here: Remote Support Features.

fields.headingSection: Eliminate Shadow IT with Efficient Integration Workflows
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
While it may not be immediately obvious, having efficient workflows for your service desk is actually a pillar of security. When your processes lack efficient workflows, it creates friction for users and admins alike. Because they are motivated to get their work done quickly, a portion of these users will naturally begin to create their own workarounds like account sharing, over-privileging accounts, and other unsecure methods that make auditing difficult.

By integrating daily or highly used tools (like Microsoft Teams) with your service desk solutions (including BeyondTrust Remote Support), your users will get to enjoy a seamless experience in a tool they’re already familiar with. Your admins will be able to easily manage ongoing support sessions and audit trails will be more streamlined.

Here’s our technical documentation on how to Enable and Customize the Microsoft Teams Integration. Please reach out to your account manager if you have any questions about this integration.

metaGlobalVars.seoTitle: Thank you for attending our masterclass!

title: GoBeyond Masterclass Recap - Privileged Remote Access

fields.headingSection: Thank you for attending our Privileged Remote Access masterclass!
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted: Download a take-home recap of session topics with relevant technical documentation.
fields.label: Download PDF

fields.headingSection: Access is Only Efficient When Secured
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
When considering access to technical systems, there should never be a tradeoff between efficiency and security. So-called “efficient” access solutions without investment in secure design and capabilities can be expensive in the long run, resulting in undetected breaches, long mitigation processes, fines for failed audits, ransom payouts for stolen data, and reputational damage.

Privileged Remote Access is designed to empower users with the secure access they need to get their jobs done. That's why we have secure-by-design features built for the enterprise.

fields.headingSection: Leveraging Vendor Groups with the Vendor Portal
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
Enabling vendor, contractor, and third-party access to your technology resources has always been a need for enterprises. However, with the globalization of teams and the fast-paced environment we live in today, it has become more ubiquitous than ever, and considered as an essential business function. Access is often delegated through poorly secured solutions like network-wide VPN access.

Make vendor access effortless with the Privileged Remote Access Vendor Portal: a one stop shop for creating and managing secure vendor access. It’s designed to alleviate administrative burdens while giving your Privileged Remote Access admins granular control over access to your technical systems. For a fully integrated experience, customize logos, email subjects and bodies, portal URLs, email domain allow lists, and more.

Find out how in our Vendor Portal Settings documentation.

fields.headingSection: Connect to Everything with Network Tunnels
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
Ditch your VPN entirely. Network Tunnels extend Privileged Remote Access to be your one access platform to rule them all.

Brand new from BeyondTrust, use Network Tunnels to expand secure access to all systems, from your cloud IT to the on-premises factory floor with familiar point-to-point VPN-like controls. Solve for every OT and IoT use case built on PLC devices and custom controllers, and anything relying on UDP or other protocol types.

Find out how to use Network Tunnels and TCP Protocol Tunnels to access remote systems here in our tech docs.

This advanced feature is included in your deployment at no cost, although is typically "disabled” by default. Get started right away by asking support (mysupport@beyondtrust.com) to switch it on.

fields.headingSection: Curious about our recent release? Find out what's new
fields.label: PRA 24.1 Release Notes

fields.headingSection: Get Hands-Free Appliance Management by Migrating to the Cloud
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
Let BeyondTrust do the heavy lifting for you when you migrate to the cloud. Hundreds of customers have already made the switch, and switching over is just a few clicks.

Take advantage of:

  • Complete feature and functionality parity
  • Automatic version updates
  • Eliminated maintenance costs
  • More APIs to leverage, and easier to leverage

Interested in learning more? Reach out to your BT rep and ask about migrating to the cloud!

title: Professional Services Cancellation Policy

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
BeyondTrust understands that schedules can change. To ensure effective planning and delivery, BeyondTrust requires at least 7 days' notice to cancel or reschedule any professional services session without penalty.

  • Cancellations or reschedules made with less than 7 days' notice will be considered late.
  • Meetings joined more than 20 minutes late will be considered cancelled.
  • Cancelling or rescheduling sessions can result in delays to the project schedule and the customer bears responsibility for missed deadlines due to late cancellations or reschedules.
  • Rescheduled sessions will be subject to resource availability and leadtimes.
  • Upon the third late cancellation or reschedule, BeyondTrust reserves the right to reduce the project scope or terminate the project.
  • Recovery of project scope may require purchase of additional professional services time.

title: BeyondTrust Remote Support SaaS Service Security Investigation

fields.headingSection: BeyondTrust Remote Support SaaS Service Security Investigation
fields.body180: Updated 1/28/25 at 5:45 p.m. EST. First published 12/8/24 at 11:00 p.m. EST.

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted: In December 2024, BeyondTrust identified a security incident that involved 17 Remote Support SaaS customers. On December 5th, 2024, a root cause analysis into a Remote Support SaaS issue identified a BeyondTrust infrastructure API key for Remote Support SaaS had been compromised and used to enable access to certain Remote Support SaaS instances by resetting local application passwords. No BeyondTrust products outside of Remote Support SaaS were affected. BeyondTrust’s forensics investigation, in coordination with a leading third-party forensics firm, was completed on January 17, 2025.

• December 5, 2024 – Anomalous behavior was confirmed and a limited number of affected instances of Remote Support SaaS were identified.
  • December 5, 2024 – Initiated incident response protocols, engaged third-party forensics firm on retainer, revoked affected API key, and quarantined infrastructure for analysis.
  • December 8, 2024 – Published initial public security advisory.
  • December 10, 2024 – Notification to Federal law enforcement partners.
  • December 13, 2024 – CVE-2024-12356 and CVE-2024-12686 zero-day vulnerabilities discovered.
  • December 14-15, 2024 – Remote Support SaaS environments patched.
  • December 16, 2024 – Critical zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2024-12356) and patches announced.
  • December 19, 2024 – Medium-severity vulnerability (CVE-2024-12686) and patches announced.
  • December 19, 2024 – Law enforcement assigns attribution to China-nexus threat actors.
  • December 20, 2024 - Present – Continue to support affected customers and law enforcement in their respective investigations.
  • January 17, 2025 – BeyondTrust Investigation completed.

BeyondTrust confirmed and began taking measures to address the security incident on December 5, 2024 that involved our Remote Support SaaS product. No BeyondTrust products outside of Remote Support SaaS were affected. No FedRAMP instances were affected. No other BeyondTrust systems were compromised, and ransomware was not involved.

Our investigation into the cause and impact of the compromise was conducted with a recognized third-party cybersecurity and forensics firm. The investigation determined that a zero-day vulnerability of a third-party application was used to gain access to an online asset in a BeyondTrust AWS account. Access to that asset then allowed the threat actor to obtain an infrastructure API key that could then be leveraged against a separate AWS account which operated Remote Support infrastructure. This vulnerability, as well as the two vulnerabilities discovered and disclosed as noted in the timeline above have been patched.

In response to the initial incident, BeyondTrust initiated our security incident response process and took actions including:

  • Immediately revoked the compromised API key.
  • Suspended and quarantined all known affected customer instances.
  • Notified affected customers and worked with them to provide alternative Remote Support SaaS instances.
  • Engaged a recognized third-party cybersecurity and forensics firm to assist in the investigation.
  • Communicated with federal law enforcement, with whom we continue to coordinate and share information.

Our forensics investigation is now complete and has identified no unauthorized access to these Remote Support SaaS instances since early December 2024. This has been confirmed by the leading third-party forensics provider who continues to scan our environment for any indicators of compromise or other signs of threat actor activity. All customers affected have been informed and continue to be actively engaged with our security teams. Additionally, our teams continue to support the ongoing law enforcement investigation.

BeyondTrust patched all SaaS instances of Remote Support and has worked to raise awareness with customers who need to do their own patching for self-hosted Remote Support instances. Further, and in addition to the Remote Support configuration guidance on our website, below are also good practices to consider when using Remote Support:

  • If self-hosted, stay up-to-date with current releases, and activate the "Apply Critical Updates Automatically" option found in the /appliance interface
  • Consider using external authentication provider (ex. SAML) over local accounts; and be sure to delete accounts not in use
  • Consider using outbound events to trigger notifications for session events
  • Integrate with a SIEM using one of our various middleware for Session Data and periodically review for suspicious activity
  • Configure syslog to send all configuration changes and authentication events to your SIEM
  • Practice least privilege for setting up user roles and capabilities, and for endpoint access
  • Periodically, review all Security Settings and leverage the Session Policy simulator to validate policies are being applied as intended
  • Periodically, review all active accounts on your appliance(s), especially those with admin privileges. Deactivate those not in use, and rotate passwords at recurring intervals where possible.
  • Enable network restrictions where possible
The following Indicators of Compromise (IOCs) were identified during our investigation.

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted: Federal law enforcement identified the unauthorized activity was attributed to a group of individuals associated with China.

Our forensics investigation is complete. We will now look to implement any necessary changes as part of our ongoing efforts to continuously strengthen our security posture. Our primary focus remains working with our affected customers’ security teams to ensure their own investigations are properly supported and concluded.

Organizations across every major industry are vulnerable to cyber threats, many of which, like these zero-day vulnerabilities, remain unknown. While our forensics investigation is complete, we know that controls and defenses should not remain static, and findings from our investigation will serve only to strengthen our security posture as we continue to implement measures to further enhance our security practices.

As always, if you have a technical issue, please open a ticket via our secure customer portal link: https://beyondtrust.com/myportal

You can also access the latest customer news and updates via our BeeKeepers community: https://beekeepers.beyondtrust.com/

rows: The investigation is complete. The 17 customers involved were all notified in early December, and BeyondTrust has since worked with them to fully support their investigations by providing artifacts, logs, indicators of compromise, and answering questions. Information has been shared to support (1) federal law enforcement efforts to pursue the threat actor and (2) threat intelligence information sharing organizations. BeyondTrust patched all SaaS instances of Remote Support and has worked to raise awareness with customers who need to do their own patching for self-hosted Remote Support instances. BeyondTrust is committed to using the findings from the investigation to further strengthen security controls and configurations holistically across its environment, and to help our customers do the same.

The forensic investigation into the Remote Support SaaS incident is approaching completion. All SaaS instances of BeyondTrust Remote Support have been fully patched against the vulnerabilities mentioned in our previous security advisories. A patch has also been pushed for self-hosted instances.

No new customers have been identified beyond those we have communicated with previously.

As a result of our on-going investigation, we identified a medium-severity vulnerability (CVE-2024-12686) within our Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access products (both self-hosted and cloud). As communicated in the 12/16/24 (AM) update, all cloud instances have been patched for this vulnerability. We have also released a patch for self-hosted versions.

We continue to pursue all possible paths as part of the forensic analysis, including our work with external forensic parties, to ensure we conduct as thorough an investigation as possible. We also continue to communicate and work closely with all known affected customers and will provide updates here until our investigation is concluded.

As part of our on-going forensics investigation into the Remote Support SaaS incident, we identified a critical vulnerability (CVE-2024-12356) within our Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access products (both self-hosted and cloud). As communicated in the 12/16/24 update, all cloud instances have been patched for this vulnerability. We have also released a patch for self-hosted versions.

This update for all self-hosted versions is a non-disruptive patch pushed through the standard update feature of the product. There is no downtime associated with this patch, and it is being pushed to self-hosted Remote Support & Privileged Remote Access customers.

We are pursuing all possible paths as part of the forensic analysis, including our work with external forensic parties, to ensure we conduct as thorough an investigation as possible. We continue to communicate and work closely with all known affected customers, and will provide updates here until our investigation is concluded.

We have proactively completed an update for our Secure Remote Access (Remote Support and Privilege Remote Access) Cloud customers, fortifying the security of their solution overall. We are pursuing all possible paths as part of the forensic analysis, including our work with external forensic parties, to ensure we conduct as thorough an investigation as possible. We continue to communicate and work closely with all known affected customers, and will provide updates here until our investigation is concluded.

In addition, we are providing recommended resources that are available through our customer portal for your solutions including:

- Remote Support Offers IP Whitelisting and Other Network Restrictions
- Monitoring Appliance Activity with Syslog
- Securing Use of the Remote Support API

While the security incident forensics investigation remains ongoing, there are no material updates to provide at this time. We continue to pursue all possible paths as part of the forensic analysis, with the assistance of external forensic parties, to ensure we conduct as thorough an investigation as possible. We continue to communicate, and work closely with, all known affected customers. We will continue to provide updates here until our investigation is concluded.

title: Secure Remote Access Solutions

fields.body180: Centrally manage and secure remote access for service desks, workers, IT admins, vendors, and infrastructure.

fields.headingMedium: Discover Secure Remote Access With a Free Trial Today

fields.heading: Try Privileged Remote Access
fields.body: Grant secure, just-in-time access with full visibility and credential protection

fields.heading: Try Remote Support
fields.body: Deliver secure, real-time support for any device, system, or global workforce

fields.headingSection: Secure Remote Access for Employees, Vendors, and Service Desks

fields.body:
Traditional remote access methods, like RDP and VPNs, lack granular access management controls and deep visibility into session activity. Their security deficiencies can leave organizations vulnerable to exploits via stolen credentials, session hijacking, and other attacks. These issues also make VPNs a particularly poor choice for vendor access.

With BeyondTrust Secure Remote Access solutions:

  • Apply robust least privilege and audit controls to all remote access from employees, vendors, and service desks.
  • Benefit from two VPN-less solutions: Privileged Remote Access and Remote Support, built for specific use cases. Each solution is a security leader in its class, while providing powerful productivity enhancement features.
  • Get comprehensive capabilities from the start, including security essentials, such as MFA, SSO, and SAML integrations.

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted: BeyondTrust Privileged Remote Access enables IT and security teams to provide users with easy-to-use, secure access to critical systems.

fields.contentCTA: {"linkedUrl":"https:\/\/www.beyondtrust.com\/forms\/cloud\/pra\/signup","linkedId":null,"linkedSiteId":null,"linkedTitle":null,"payload":"{\"customText\":\"Sign up for a Free Privileged Remote Access Trial\"}","type":"url"}

fields.heading: Secure Remote Access
fields.body: Connect securely to critical IT systems, cloud applications, and OT environments without relying on a VPN. Every session, whether human or non-human, is authenticated, authorized, and fully auditable.

fields.heading: Session Management
fields.body: Maintain full visibility and control with detailed logs of every privileged session. Automatically record actions and permissions to simplify compliance.

fields.heading: Privileged Access Control
fields.body: Apply least privilege and just-in-time access by granting users only the remote access they need when they need it. Extend the same protections to non-human identities to reduce risk and prevent misuse.

fields.heading: Flexible Consoles & Tools
fields.body: Keep workflows efficient with familiar tools like PuTTY and Azure Data Studio, while enabling access from mobile or web consoles.

fields.heading: Streamlined Authentication
fields.body: Boost productivity and security with MFA, passwordless options, and SAML integration designed for fast onboarding and seamless access.

fields.heading: Compliance & Session Auditing
fields.body: Simplify compliance with SOC 2 ready audit trails, forensic insights, and advanced analytics available in real time or after each session.

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted: BeyondTrust Secure Remote Access is engineered with robust security controls and helps enable zero trust principles, zero trust architectures (ZTA), and zero trust network access (ZTNA) by enforcing least privilege, implementing segmentation, isolating assets, and monitoring and managing sessions. The solution also enables a just-in-time access model, with MFA and the option for per-session MFA, to reduce risk related to standing privileges.
fields.label: Learn more

fields.heading: FedRAMP® Authorized Secure Remote Access Solution
fields.body: BeyondTrust is FedRAMP® authorized to operate (ATO) at the moderate impact level for its Secure Remote Access (SRA) solutions, which include Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access. Your organization can fulfill the Federal Government's Cloud Computing Mandate by using BeyondTrust's FedRAMP® Moderate authorized solutions.

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted: BeyondTrust Remote Support is designed to streamline and better secure the day-to-day workflow of internal and customer-facing help and service desks. Seamless remote support capabilities along with chat, video, and customizable approval and notification tools, make life easier for technicians.

fields.contentCTA: {"linkedUrl":"http:\/\/www.beyondtrust.com\/secure-remote-access#trial-rs-single","linkedId":null,"linkedSiteId":null,"linkedTitle":null,"payload":"{\"customText\":\"Sign up for a Free Remote Support Trial\"}","type":"url"}

fields.heading: Remote Access Control
fields.body: Access, diagnose, and fix devices on or off the corporate network, and deliver support with thick client, browser-based, or mobile access.

fields.heading: Efficiency & Scale
fields.body: Support growth with automation, mass deployment, and escalation tools, now with orchestration and permission controls.

fields.heading: Vaulted Credentials
fields.body: Discover, store, rotate, and inject up to 100,000 credentials securely through the Vault.

fields.heading: Real-Time Dashboard
fields.body: Monitor sessions, agents, and AI activity in a unified view with customizable metrics and shareable reports.

fields.heading: Jump Clients
fields.body: Enable unattended system access at scale with mass deployments and just-in-time controls.

fields.heading: Customization & User Experience Controls
fields.body: Personalize portals, invitations, watermarks, and surveys while branding your deployment’s unique URL and client.

fields.heading: Auditing & Compliance
fields.body: Maintain an indisputable audit trail with real-time reporting, detailed video logs, and session data.

fields.heading: Integrations
fields.body: Integrate Remote Support with your favorite CRM, ITSM, SIEM tools, and leverage our open API to create your own AI tooling.

fields.headingSection: Secure Remote Support for Service Desks
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
Service desks need more complete remote support solutions to solve an expanding list of use cases while meeting high standards for issue resolution. In addition, service desks are targeted by attackers because of their sensitive access and need robust security features that won’t take valuable time and resources to implement.

BeyondTrust Remote Support is a single, holistic solution built to support every device and improve service desk workflows, while also providing robust security. Enable your help desk and ITSM teams to:

  • Securely access and fix any remote device, on any platform, anywhere in the world.
  • Adhere to ITSM best practices.
  • Work seamlessly with technologies such as ServiceNow, Jira Service Manager, Zendesk, and Microsoft Teams, improving both the user and technician experience.
fields.label: Learn more

fields.question: What is secure remote access?
fields.answer: Secure remote access allows users to connect to systems, networks, and applications from anywhere while enforcing strong authentication, least privilege, and continuous monitoring. It helps replace insecure methods like VPNs or RDP.

fields.question: What are the features of secure remote access solutions?
fields.answer: BeyondTrust Secure Remote Access enforces Zero Trust by granting just-in-time access, supporting approval workflows, vaulting and injecting credentials, recording and auditing sessions, tunneling protocols, automating tasks with scripts, and integrating MFA and SSO. These capabilities replace VPNs and strengthen security by controlling and monitoring every connection.

fields.question: What is BeyondTrust Secure Remote Access?
fields.answer:
BeyondTrust Secure Remote Access includes two solutions:

  • Privileged Remote Access (PRA): Delivers secure, VPN-less access for internal and external users with least privilege controls and integration into existing workflows.

  • Remote Support (RS): Enables service desks and support teams to access and support any device, on or off the network, with full auditing.

Both solutions enforce least privilege, monitor every session, and reduce attack surfaces while maintaining seamless connectivity.

fields.question: Is BeyondTrust a Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) solution?
fields.answer: Yes. BeyondTrust Secure Remote Access helps enable ZTNA by enforcing least privilege, segmenting access, and monitoring every session. It replaces VPNs by providing VPN-less connectivity, supports credential injection (without exposing secrets), and ensures audited, controlled access only when needed.

fields.question: Is BeyondTrust a VPN?
fields.answer: No, BeyondTrust is not a VPN. It provides VPN-less secure remote access, using Privileged Remote Access and Remote Support solutions to control, monitor, and secure connections without exposing networks.

fields.question: What is BeyondTrust Remote Jump Client?
fields.answer: The BeyondTrust Jump Client is a lightweight agent that lets authorized users securely access and manage remote systems, even when those systems are unattended or outside the corporate network.

fields.question: How doe BeyondTrust Privileged Remote Access work?
fields.answer: BeyondTrust Privileged Remote Access (PRA) delivers secure, centralized access for employees and third-party vendors to critical systems without relying on VPNs or direct connections. It enforces least privilege with just-in-time access, injects credentials without exposing them to users, and records and audits every session for visibility and compliance.

fields.question: How does BeyondTrust Remote Support work?
fields.answer: BeyondTrust Remote Support allows service desks and IT teams to securely connect to any device or system, on any platform, from anywhere. It integrates with ITSM tools and enforces access controls, session monitoring, and audit trails.

metaGlobalVars.seoDescription: Protect every remote session for employees, vendors, and service desks. BeyondTrust unifies ZTNA and PAM security with no VPN required.

title: Meeting Today's Identity Security Challenges with Modern PAM

fields.heading: Meeting Today's Identity Security Challenges with Modern PAM

fields.headingSection: Reduce Risk, Decrease Cost, and Optimize Productivity
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
Most organizations today struggle to find elevated permissions and right-size access across their hybrid IT environments, let alone achieve it without inserting convoluted workflows that add friction and impair productivity.

While Privileged Access Management (PAM) has traditionally focused on protecting accounts with privileged access, what’s considered “privileged” isn’t so straightforward today. Now, almost every identity has Paths to Privilege™ that need to be understood and protected.

Organizations clearly need a more agile and comprehensive approach. Modern PAM can step in to address these critical identity security gaps, while also optimizing access workflows to improve end-user and admin productivity.


Watch the video and learn:

  • How visibility and management gaps are leaving organizations exposed to threats that cross domains.
  • What the concepts True Privilege and Paths to Privilege are, and why understanding and addressing them is central to modern identity security success.
  • BeyondTrust’s groundbreaking approach to Modern PAM, and how it minimizes the attack surface, slashes help desk tickets, and helps empower end-user productivity.

title: Menghadapi Tantangan Keamanan Identitas Saat Ini dengan PAM Modern

fields.heading: Menghadapi Tantangan Keamanan Identitas Saat Ini dengan PAM Modern

fields.headingSection: Kurangi Risiko, Turunkan Biaya, dan Optimalkan Produktivitas
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
Sebagian besar organisasi saat ini kesulitan dalam mengidentifikasi dan menentukan tingkat akses secara tepat di seluruh lingkungan TI hybrid mereka, apalagi mencapainya tanpa memasukkan alur kerja yang rumit yang mana akan menambah hambatan dan mengganggu produktivitas.

Meskipun Privileged Access Management (PAM) secara tradisional berfokus pada perlindungan akun-akun dengan akses istimewa, definisi “akses istimewa” saat ini tidak lagi sesederhana itu. Kini, hampir setiap identitas memiliki Paths to Privilege™ yang perlu dipahami dan dilindungi.

Organisasi jelas membutuhkan pendekatan yang lebih gesit dan menyeluruh. PAM modern dapat mengatasi celah keamanan identitas yang krusial ini, sekaligus mengoptimalkan alur kerja akses guna meningkatkan produktivitas pengguna akhir dan administrator.

Tonton video ini dan pelajari:

  • Bagaimana kurangnya visibilitas dan celah dalam operasional membuat organisasi rentan terhadap ancaman lintas domain.
  • Apa itu konsep True Privilege dan Paths to Privilege, serta mengapa memahami dan menanganinya sangat penting untuk keberhasilan keamanan identitas modern.
  • Pendekatan BeyondTrust yang inovatif terhadap PAM Modern, dan bagaimana pendekatan ini meminimalkan permukaan serangan, mengurangi tiket help desk, serta meningkatkan produktivitas pengguna akhir.

title: 현대적인 PAM으로 오늘날의 아이덴티티 보안 과제에 대응하기

fields.heading: 현대적인 PAM으로 오늘날의 아이덴티티 보안 과제에 대응하기

fields.headingSection: 위험 감소, 비용 절감, 그리고 생산성 최적화
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
오늘날 대부분의 조직은 하이브리드 IT 환경 전반에서 높은 권한을 식별하고 적절한 수준의 접근 권한을 설정하는 데 어려움을 겪고 있습니다. 복잡한 워크플로우를 도입하지 않고 이를 달성하는 것은 말할 것도 없이 어려운 것이지만, 이러한 워크플로우는 마찰을 가중시키고 생산성을 저하시킬 수 있습니다.

전통적으로 권한 접근 관리(PAM)는 권한 접근이 있는 계정을 보호하는 데 중점을 두었지만, 오늘날 “주요권한”으로 간주되는 범위는 그렇게 단순한 것은 아닙니다. 이제 거의 모든 아이덴티티에는 이해되고 보호해야 할 *Paths to Privilege™가 있습니다.

조직은 보다 민첩하고 포괄적인 접근 방식이 필요합니다. 최신의 PAM은 이러한 중요한 아이덴티티 보안의 격차를 해결하는 동시에 액세스 워크플로우를 최적화하여 최종 사용자 및 관리자의 생산성을 향상시킬 수 있습니다.

영상을 시청하고 알아보세요:

  • 가시성과 관리의 격차로 인해 조직이 여러 도메인을 넘나드는 위협에 어떻게 노출되는지를 알아보세요.
  • True Privilege와 Paths to Privilege 개념이 무엇이며, 이러한 개념을 이해하고 대응하는 것이 왜 최신의 성공적인 아이덴티티 보안에 중요한지 알아보세요.
  • BeyondTrust의 혁신적인 최신의 PAM 접근 방식이 어떻게 공격 표면을 최소화하고 헬프데스크 티켓을 줄이며, 최종 사용자의 생산성을 향상시키는지를 확인해보세요.

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted: Got an idea for a new feature? Maybe a tweak to make something work even better? Wish there was an integration with another product to make you even more productive? You've come to the right place. The Product Ideas Portal lets you submit whatever product feedback you have: good, bad, ugly, and anywhere between.

title: Attack Vectors

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
Developed by Morey J. Haber, the Attack Vector series spans major cyberattack vectors to help educate IT leaders about the modern threat landscape and how to mitigate cyber risk.

Request Morey as a guest speaker below.

fields.heading: Morey J. Haber
fields.body:
Morey J. Haber is the Chief Security Advisor at BeyondTrust. As the Chief Security Advisor, Morey is the lead identity and technical evangelist at BeyondTrust. He has more than 25 years of IT industry experience and has authored five books: Attack Vectors: The History of Cybersecurity, Identity Attack Vectors, Privileged Attack Vectors, Asset Attack Vectors, and Cloud Attack Vectors. Morey has previously served as BeyondTrust’s Chief Security Officer, Chief Technology Officer, and Vice President of Product Management during his nearly 12-year tenure with the company.

Morey regularly contributes to the growth of the IT security industry through his contributions to:

  • Transparency in Cyber (founding member)
  • Identity Defined Security Alliance (IDSA) (Executive Advisory Board member)

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
The Attack Vector series provides a detailed examination of common and emerging threat vectors that are increasingly being exploited by threat actors and used in attacks on organizations. The purpose of the series is to arm IT leaders with the insights and best practices they need to better manage cyber risk.

The series consists of 5 books:

  • Attack Vectors: The History of Cybersecurity (2025) – Details the evolution of cyberattacks over the past several decades, including the evolution from early worms to AI-powered deepfakes, how cybersecurity solutions have adapted in response, and why understanding the past is critical to responding to today’s most sophisticated cyber threats.
  • Identity Attack Vectors (2024, 2020) – The first edition (2020) details the risks associated with poor identity management practices, the techniques that threat actors and insiders leverage, and the operational best practices that organizations should adopt to protect against identity theft and account compromises, and to develop an effective identity governance program. The revised second edition (2024) expands on these areas to uncover how inadequate identity and privileged access controls can be exploited to compromise accounts and credentials within an organization, and how role-based identity assignments, entitlements, and auditing strategies can be used to mitigate modern identity threats across an organization's entire Identity Fabric.
  • Cloud Attack Vectors (2022) – Details the risks associated with cloud deployments, the techniques threat actors leverage, the empirically-tested defensive measures organizations should adopt, and how to improve detection of malicious activity.
  • Privileged Attack Vectors (2020, 2017) – The first edition (2017) details the risks associated with poor privilege management, the techniques that threat actors leverage, and the defensive measures that organizations should adopt to protect against an incident, protect against lateral movement, and improve the ability to detect malicious activity due to the inappropriate usage of privileged credentials. The revised and expanded second edition (2020) also covers new attack vectors, has updated definitions for privileged access management (PAM), new strategies for defense, tested empirical steps for a successful implementation, and includes new disciplines for least-privilege endpoint management and privileged remote access.
  • Asset Attack Vectors (2018) – Details how to build an enterprise-class vulnerability management program, drawing on proven techniques for threat analysis, risk measurement, and regulatory reporting. Asset Attack Vectors also outlines practical service level agreements (SLAs) for vulnerability management and patch management.

The History of Cybersecurity

"The history of cyberattacks is more than a tale of cunning threat actors and unsuspecting victims; it’s the blueprint by which we forge our strategies for tomorrow."

— Morey J. Haber, Attack Vectors

fields.body:
Who this book is for:

New security management professionals, auditors, and information technology staff looking to understand the history of cybersecurity.

What you'll learn:

  • The history of cybersecurity from the early 1950’s to present.
  • An exploration of the terminology that has defined the threat landscape.
  • The history of malware, exploits, breaches, syndicates, and people throughout the last 25 years, and what learnings we can continue to apply to modern and emerging threats.
  • How modern cybersecurity solutions have been developed to address the evolution of attack vectors.
  • Best practices for addressing cyber risks
  • How to best poise your organization to defend against emerging threats, while confidently and securely embracing the next wave of digital transformation.

Authored by: Morey J. Haber

Buy it now from Amazon

Buy it now from Apress

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
Strategically Designing and Implementing Identity Security

"It is easier for a threat actor to login versus hack in." — Morey J. Haber, Identity Attack Vectors

fields.body:
Who this book is for:

Management and implementers in IT operations, security, and auditing looking to understand and implement an Identity and Access Management (IAM) program, manage privileges in these environments, and harden their identity attack surface.

What you'll learn:

  • The concepts behind an identity and how its associated credentials and accounts can be leveraged as an attack vector
  • How to implement an effective identity security strategy to manage identities and accounts based on roles and entitlements, including the most sensitive privileged accounts
  • Where identity security controls play a part in the cyber kill chain and how privileges should be managed as a potential weak link
  • How to build upon industry standards and strategies, such as Zero Trust, to integrate key identity security technologies into a corporate ecosystem
  • How to plan for a successful identity and access security deployment based on real-world strategies to prevent identity attack vectors

Authored by: Morey J. Haber and D. Rolls

Buy it now from Amazon

Buy it now from Apress

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
Building Effective Cyber-Defense Strategies to Protect Cloud Resources

"No single, standalone solution or strategy can effectively protect against all cloud attack vectors or identify all malicious activity." —Morey J. Haber, Cloud Attack Vectors

fields.body:
Who this book is for:

New security professionals, entry-level cloud security engineers, managers embarking on digital transformation, and auditors looking to understand security and compliance risks associated with the cloud.

What you'll learn:

  • The key definitions pertaining to cloud technologies, threats, and cybersecurity solutions
  • How entitlements, permissions, rights, identities, accounts, credentials, and exploits can be leveraged to breach a cloud environment
  • How to implement defensive and monitoring strategies to mitigate cloud threats, including those unique to cloud and hybrid cloud environments
  • How to develop a comprehensive model for documenting risk, compliance, and reporting based on your cloud implementation

Authored by: Morey J. Haber and Chris Hills

Buy it now from Amazon

Buy it now from Apress

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
Building Effective Cyber-Defense Strategies to Protect Organizations

"Privileges, insecure passwords, administrative rights, and remote access can be combined as an attack vector to breach any organization." —Morey J. Haber, Privileged Attack Vectors

fields.body:
Who this book is for:

Security management professionals, new security professionals, and auditors looking to understand and solve privilege access management problems.

What you'll learn:

  • How identities, accounts, credentials, passwords, and exploits can be leveraged to escalate privileges during an attack
  • How to implement defensive and monitoring strategies to mitigate privileged threats and risk
  • A 10-step privilege management implementation plan to guide you through a successful privilege access management journey
  • How to develop a comprehensive model for documenting risk, compliance, and reporting based on privilege session activity.

Authored by: Morey J. Haber

Buy it now from Amazon

Buy it now from Apress

fields.headingSection: Building Effective Vulnerability Management Strategies to Protect Organizations
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted: "In the modern enterprise, everything connected to the network is a target." — Morey J. Haber, Asset Attack Vectors

fields.body:
Who this book is for:

New and intermediate security management professionals, auditors, and information technology staff looking to build an effective vulnerability management program and defend against asset-based cyberattacks.

What you’ll learn:

  • How to create comprehensive assessment and risk identification policies and procedures
  • How to implement a complete vulnerability management workflow in nine easy steps
  • The implications of active, dormant, and carrier vulnerability states
  • How to develop, deploy, and maintain custom and commercial vulnerability management programs
  • The best strategies for vulnerability remediation, mitigation, and removal
  • Hot to automate credentialed scans that leverage least-privilege access principles
  • Read real-world examples from case studies that share successful strategies and reveal potential pitfalls.

Authored by: Morey J. Haber and B. Hibbert

Buy it now from Amazon

Buy it now from Apress

fields.heading: Decoding the Future with Cybersecurity’s Past: A Conversation with Morey J. Haber
fields.body: In this Q&A style blog, Morey J. Haber shares an overview of research and insights from his latest book: Attack Vectors: The History of Cybersecurity. He covers why understanding cybersecurity history is crucial for protecting our future, and which trends and changes have occurred throughout the history of cyberattacks—including the pivot from technological exploits to identity theft. Read on to learn how lessons from the past can help us address today's emerging threats related to AI adoption, account poisoning, geolocation trackers, and more.

fields.heading: New Identity Attack Vectors Book: A Q&A With The Authors
fields.body: In this Q&A style blog, we sit down with authors of the latest second edition of Identity Attack Vectors, Morey and Darran, to explore the thought process that went into the writing of the book—and to learn more about why detecting and defending against identity threats should be the basis of all modern cybersecurity initiatives. Read on to hear from Morey and Darran exploring how identity security has emerged as a cornerstone of modern enterprise security, and much more.

fields.heading: Identity Attacks: The New Frontier of Cybersecurity
fields.body: In this session, join BeyondTrust’s Chief Security Advisor, Morey Haber and Founder of Cloud 10, Darran Rolls authors of the second edition of "Identity Attack Vectors," as they delve into the evolving landscape of identity-based threats and explore practical strategies for defense.

fields.heading: Inside Cloud Security Threats and Attack Vectors
fields.body:
An interview with the authors of Cloud Attack Vectors

The shift to the cloud in enterprise computing has contributed to an evolution of the cybersecurity landscape—one that has pushed it well beyond the boundaries of the traditional perimeter. Work from anywhere, shadow cloud IT, overextended VPNs and remote access—these new challenges are all contributing to a continuous and exponential increase of attack vectors that are adding to the threats organizations have been battling against for years. Read on for a conversation with the authors as we explore the thought process that went into the writing of the book.

fields.heading: Cloud Attack Vectors: Build Cyber-Defense Strategies to Protect Cloud Resources
fields.body: In this webcast session, cybersecurity experts and co-authors of Cloud Attack Vectors offer best practices for addressing cloud attack vectors. Learn cutting-edge strategies for building the optimal cloud defense for your organization’s unique cloud environment.

fields.heading: Identity Attack Vectors Book Pairs Insights from Two IT Security Leaders
fields.body:
An overview of Identity Attack Vectors

Almost every successful (that means the threat actors win) cyberattack today exploits identity as an attack vector. In particular, privileged access management (PAM)—a key component of identity and governance administration (IGA)—plays a critical role in the attack chain. While part of the identity security challenge is technological, another part is organizational. At many enterprises, identity management and security run as parallel and separate entities, each with their own teams, budgets, and priorities. Read this blog for key highlights from the book and an overview of what organizations need to do to ensure the most basic levels of security for corporate identities and assets.

fields.heading: New Privileged Attack Vectors Book: Q&A with Author Morey Haber
fields.body:
An interview with the Author of Privileged Attack Vectors

While privileged access posed a security threat for decades, it’s only in the last 5-7 years that the privileged attack surface has exploded and become the most dangerous IT security threat. Yet, a knowledge gap existed with regards to understanding the scope of privileged threats and how to programmatically address that prodigious risk. In the completely revised and expanded second edition of Privileged Attack Vectors reflects the significantly changing world of Privileged Access Management (PAM). Read on for a summary of what is covered in the latest edition of the book, followed by an insightful Q&A on the book, cybersecurity and PAM trends, and more with the author himself!

fields.heading: New Book "Asset Attack Vectors"... And How to Prepare the Right Defensive Strategies
fields.body: Today’s network environments are dynamic and perimeters are expanding, requiring multiple layers of defense to mitigate vulnerabilities and stop data breaches. In the modern enterprise, everything connected to the network and cloud is a target. In Privileged Attack Vectors, Morey Haber and Brad Hibbert explain how to build an effective vulnerability management strategy to protect an organization’s assets, applications, and data. Read this blog for an overview of the book and the key highlights that can help you build a vulnerability management program designed to work in the modern threat environment.

fields.bodyMedium: Request Morey Haber, Chris Hills, or a subject matter expert from BeyondTrust to speak at your next event. Contact us to receive more information.

metaGlobalVars.seoDescription: The Attack Vectors series of books covers critical cybersecurity disciplines, providing insights and best practices from the top subject matter experts.

title: PartnerTrust Roadshow – Join Us

fields.headingSection: Perfect Fit. Perfect Alignment. Together We’re Unstoppable.
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
These exclusive partner sessions are built to help you strengthen relationships with your local BeyondTrust team, drive deeper co-selling collaboration, and expand identity security opportunities across your accounts. You’ll gain access to partner-only promotions, FY26 GTM and product roadmap insights, along with alignment updates with key Technology Alliances Partners.

We’ll conclude the day with an exclusive Indochino custom shirt fitting, offering personalized measurements, premium fabrics, and a white-glove tailoring experience—so you leave not only with strategic insights, but with a shirt crafted precisely for you.

Find the closest city near you and register today! We look forward to seeing you and sharing this exclusive experience.

title: BeyondTrust Customer Advocacy Program

fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
The BeyondTrust Customer Advocacy Program (CAP) empowers our most passionate customers to maximize the value of their BeyondTrust investment by sharing insights, shaping innovation, and gaining recognition for their security and success. The CAP is a flexible, opt-in program designed for customers who want to celebrate their achievements, build their personal brand, and deepen connections with the BeyondTrust team and the wider customer community.

Choose your own adventure! Participation is always on your terms, with no requirement to be named publicly. Advocates can engage in both private and public advocacy activities that align with their goals and organizational guidelines. We only ask that you participate in at least 1 activity per calendar year to maintain advocate status.

fields.headingSection: Celebrate Your Loyalty & Success with Exclusive Advocate Perks & Rewards
fields.bodyRedactorRestricted:
• Exclusive CAP merchandise & welcome kit
  • VIP experience at company-hosted events
  • Recognition across BeyondTrust channels
  • Annual Advocate of the Year eligibility
  • Press & media opportunities
  • Limited-edition advocate swag, gifts, & surprise mailers
  • Certificates, digital badges & custom trophies to showcase your advocate status

• Speaking Opportunities: Showcase your expertise by representing your organization at industry events, conferences, or panel discussions, highlighting your success with our solutions.
  • Virtual Webinars, Podcast, & User Groups: Position yourself as a thought leader by joining a virtual event to share your insights, experiences, and results with a trusted audience of customer peers.
  • Case Studies: Build your personal brand through a written or video case study detailing your business challenges, implementation journey, and the outcomes you achieved for your organization.
  • Peer-to-Peer Reference Calls: Serve as a trusted voice by speaking confidentially with prospects about your product experience and our partnership.
  • Product Feedback: Shape the future of identity security by providing feedback, participating in beta programs, and influencing the BeyondTrust roadmap.
  • And so much more!

For more information, reach out to customeradvocacy@beyondtrust.com.
